cityDNA.coreFantasy: A cold-water sealfolk harbor civilization where blue lanterns, shelter halls, cargo yards, public works, nursery basins, and harbor ritual make endurance civic.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Frostharbor survives because it turns care, warmth, tide knowledge, logistics, and nursery protection into public architecture.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Frostharbor turns cold sea, ice, shelter, cargo logistics, tide surveys, nursery waters, shell spirals, and blue lanterns into a settled amphibious city of protection.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Wet stone, ice-worn masonry, dark timber, rope, iron fittings, blue glass, shell inlay, slate roofs, shelter halls, seawalls, causeways, cargo yards, nursery basins, and lantern-lit harbor fog.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A sealfolk city of weather-hardened citizens who treat shelter, tide knowledge, cargo order, nursery protection, safe passage, and communal warmth as civic virtues.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Blue lanterns, shell spirals, ice tides, roundhouses, shelter pillars, nursery pools, cargo ropes, harbor arches, and tide seals symbolize survival through shared protection.

canonLocks: Frostharbor is a mature cold-water sealfolk harbor civilization, not a temporary fishing village.
The Wonder is the Sealfolk Ice-Tide Roundhouse of Blue Lanterns.
The Beacon is the Shelter Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is the Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel.
The five primary districts are Shelter Hall, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, Nursery / Spawning Grounds, and Harbor Campus.

The uploaded district anchors include S1 Shelter Hall, S1 Shelter Pillar, S1 Shelter Kiosk, Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Inventory Hall Nook, Public Works Authority Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, S5 Harbor Campus, S5 Harbor Arch, and S5 Harbor Bridgelet.
provisionalCanon: Frostharbor's highest civic virtue is shelter.
The central tension is home sea versus hostile sea.
The shelter district is the emotional equivalent of the city's front door: cold water enters civic life there and becomes communal warmth.
rejectedReads: Frostharbor is only a cold port.
Sealfolk adaptation is decorative rather than shaping streets, housing, logistics, and public care.
Shelter and nursery spaces can be generalized into ordinary harbor buildings.
cautionFlags: Keep ramps, basins, water steps, low platforms, and amphibious movement visible.
Do not hide logistics; cargo and warehousing are civic power.
Make cold, wet conditions normal operating conditions, not one-off weather flavor.
Preserve exact shelter and harbor sidecar names rather than smoothing them into generic labels.

environment.environmentalLogic: Frostharbor builds a civic membrane between cold sea and settlement: docks, basins, shelters, causeways, roundhouses, seawalls, and blue lanterns make harsh water habitable.

skyline: Sealfolk Ice-Tide Roundhouse of Blue Lanterns as broad civic wonder anchor
Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel as water-facing authority
Shelter Hall Beacon as public signal landmark
Harbor Basilica, Survey Hall, blue lanterns, and low water-facing massing along the shoreline

civilization.people: Citizens are sealfolk: physically competent in cold water, practical around weather, communal around warmth, and fluent in tide, cargo, shelter, and nursery routines.
civilization.governance: Authority is protective-logistical: the Citadel governs tide law, harbor command, shelter readiness, public works, sealed records, cargo order, and emergency weather response.
civilization.religion: Religious expression is harbor-centered and tide-aware, carried through shell spirals, blue lanterns, harbor basilica rites, nursery blessings, storm remembrance, and roundhouse gatherings.

streets.appearance: Wet, cold, low, and water-facing: slick stone, timber docks, causeways, ramps, blue lanterns, shelter halls, cargo crates, nursery basins, ropes, harbor arches, and bridgelets.
streets.movement: Movement is amphibious and logistical through boats, sledges, dock carts, ramps, bridges, water steps, cargo paths, shelter routes, and low platforms.
streets.lighting: Blue lanterns and shelter fires define safety; cold daylight reflects from water, wet stone, ice, and pale harbor buildings.
streets.weatherEffects: Fog, sleet, snow, sea spray, partial visibility, lantern halos, and muffled bells are normal operating conditions.
streets.dayCycle: Morning begins with fog, lantern checks, tide readings, cargo unloading, permit work, fish markets, and nursery tending.
streets.nightCycle: Night is blue lanterns across dark water, shelter hall warmth, guarded docks, storm watches, and quiet nursery basins.

housing.wealthy: Water-facing insulated roundhouses and citadel-adjacent homes with shell windows, blue lamps, private docks, thick doors, and warm common rooms.
housing.middle: Dock-family houses, warehouse apartments, boatwright rooms, shelter-adjacent flats, drying racks, rope rails, and dual street-water entries.
housing.poor: Low harbor-edge rooms, shared hearths, wet entries, cramped dockside quarters, shelter-kiosk reliance, and practical storage above flood level.

districts.name: Shelter Hall
districts.civicPurpose: Communal warmth, emergency gathering, beacon response, and public readiness.
districts.visualIdentity: S1 Shelter Hall, Shelter Hall Beacon, S1 Shelter Pillar, S1 Shelter Kiosk, blue lanterns, warm halls, wet thresholds, and public gathering platforms.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Protective, warm against cold, and immediately civic.

districts.name: Warehousing / Logistics
districts.civicPurpose: Cargo movement, inventory, dock labor, cold-chain organization, and harbor commerce.
districts.visualIdentity: Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Inventory Hall Nook, crates, cranes, ropes, sledges, wet docks, and warehouse fronts.

districts.name: Public Works Authority
districts.civicPurpose: Tide measurement, seawall maintenance, permits, weather warnings, and technical harbor governance.
districts.visualIdentity: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, seawall tools, tide gauges, wet maps, and civic workers.

districts.name: Nursery / Spawning Grounds
districts.civicPurpose: Protected life-cycle space, family continuity, warm basin care, and communal vulnerability.
districts.visualIdentity: Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, protected water, low walls, caregivers, and soft blue light.

districts.name: Harbor Campus
districts.civicPurpose: Harbor ritual, public mourning, maritime blessing, civic-water identity, and ceremonial crossing.
districts.visualIdentity: S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet, blue lamps, shell forms, wet stone plazas, and sea-facing ritual approaches.

wonder.name: Sealfolk Ice-Tide Roundhouse of Blue Lanterns
wonder.symbolicRole: Communal shelter against cold water, tide, storm, and uncertainty.
wonder.visualRole: Massive roundhouse complex of wet stone, shell forms, blue lanterns, tide platforms, shelter halls, and broad harbor massing.
wonder.civicRole: Citywide gathering place for storm shelter, winter festivals, emergency musters, lantern ceremonies, and community meals.
storyHooks: A storm drives every district toward the Roundhouse at once.

beacon.name: Shelter Hall Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public signal tied to shelter, readiness, and communal response.
beacon.visualRole: Tall blue-lit signal tower integrated with shelter halls, visible through fog and reflected in harbor water.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates shelter routing, storm warnings, public announcements, emergency gathering, and lantern maintenance.
storyHooks: A Beacon change makes every shelter, dock, and family route recalculate at once.

seatOfPower.name: Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Tide law, sealed records, harbor command, and shell-spiral continuity made government.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Water-facing citadel of pale wet stone, shell spiral windows, blue-lit chambers, command terraces, sealed archives, and tide platforms.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Governs harbor operations, archive sealing, tide-law hearings, public works command, and emergency weather response.
storyHooks: A tide record in the Citadel decides whether the harbor can open before a storm.

majorLandmarks.name: Cargo Yard
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Crates, cranes, ropes, sledges, wet docks, inventory halls, and labor lines.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Cargo sorting, inventory control, dock labor, storage, and cold-chain harbor organization.
storyHooks: A missing crate reveals who controls the city's shelter supplies.

majorLandmarks.name: Survey Hall
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Technical knowledge that keeps the harbor alive.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Tide gauges, wet maps, blue lamps, permit yard, seawall tools, and survey platforms.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Public works, tide measurements, weather warnings, permits, and seawall maintenance.
storyHooks: A survey warning is ignored because cargo profit depends on delay.

majorLandmarks.name: Nursery Basin
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Protected warm water basin with shell inlay, low walls, caregivers, family routes, and soft blue light.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Life-cycle care, nursery protection, family rituals, and communal supervision.
storyHooks: A nursery warning changes the moral weight of a harbor decision.

majorLandmarks.name: Harbor Basilica
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Sea labor and civic reverence joined in ritual architecture.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Basilica-like harbor structure with arches, bridgelets, shell forms, blue lamps, and water-facing approaches.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Harbor ritual, blessings, public mourning, maritime ceremonies, and civic-water identity.
storyHooks: A harbor blessing becomes a public admission of danger.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show S1 Shelter Hall, Shelter Hall Beacon, S1 Shelter Pillar, S1 Shelter Kiosk, blue lanterns, fire benches, and wet arrivals.
S2 must show Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Inventory Hall Nook, crates, cranes, dockworkers, and rope coils.
S3 must show Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, tide gauges, surveyors, and seawall tools.
S4 must show Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, warm water, caregivers, and family groups.
S5 must show S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, S5 Harbor Bridgelet, ritual crowds, blue lamps, and shell details.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Use wet stone, blue lanterns, fog, ropes, low halls, shell spirals, cargo yards, tide gauges, nursery basins, shelter fires, and harbor ritual architecture.

The Sealfolk Ice-Tide Roundhouse of Blue Lanterns should be the wonder-scale harbor anchor; the Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel should be water-facing authority; the Shelter Hall Beacon should be the civic safety signal.
requiredVisualElements: Sealfolk Ice-Tide Roundhouse of Blue Lanterns
elementsToAvoid: shelter and nursery spaces treated as background
